title: Report a Maintenance Issue
summary: Report a maintenance problem to a landlord or building manager in a clear and actionable way.
learnerPromise: You will be able to describe a maintenance problem clearly, include the details needed for a quick fix, and send the report through the right channel.
whyItMatters: Clear reports get faster repairs and help build a good relationship with your landlord or building manager. Vague reports often lead to delays and follow-up questions.
realLifeUses: Report a leaking faucet before it causes water damage
Let your landlord know the heater is not working when temperatures drop
Document a broken appliance so it can be repaired or replaced
Explain a recurring issue with enough detail that it gets fixed the first time
materialsNeeded: Phone, email, or maintenance portal access
Notes or photos of the problem
result: A written or spoken maintenance report that includes what, where, when, and how urgent the issue is.
youWillLearn: Describe a problem in one or two clear sentences
Include location, when it started, and how serious it feels
Choose the right way to send the report (email, portal, phone, etc.)
Follow up politely if you do not hear back within a reasonable time
miniChallenge: Pick a small issue in your home (real or imagined) and write a short maintenance report that says what it is, where it is, and when it started.
steps: Look at the problem and note exactly where it is and what is happening
Take a photo if possible and write down when you first noticed it
Open your lease or building info to find the correct contact method
Write or say: what the problem is, where it is, when it started, and how urgent it feels
Send the report and note the date so you can follow up if needed
completionCriteria: Your report includes what, where, and when for the problem
You send it through the channel your landlord or building manager prefers
commonProblems: It can be hard to tell if something is your responsibility or the landlord's
Some landlords are slow to respond even to well-written reports
Urgent issues may need a phone call instead of an email or portal message
tips: Keep your report short and factual—avoid emotional language
Photos help a lot, especially for leaks, mold, or damage
Always note the date you sent the report for your records
If it is urgent (no heat in winter, major leak), call instead of emailing
